content.full_text:
.E
FN,
o
Republican
National
Committee
Benlamln L. Ginsberg
Chiel Counsel
lllchael A. Hess
Janico P. Lacy
Deputy Chief Counsels
July 23, 1990
scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Califomia Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Offrce Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
RE: Advice l-etter Request
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
This is to request an advice letter pursuant to Cal. Code $ 83114@) concerning whether
the Republican National Committee ('RNC') can establish a Proposition 73 qualified
recipient committee under 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18539.
The RNC is a national political party committee established and maintained by the
Republican Party. The RNC seeks to establish a California political committee which
would be considered a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee. Regulation 18539
expressly permits state and local committees to establish such committees, but is silent
as to whether a national political committee may also do so. See $18539 (a)(1). While
Section 18539 as adopted by the Commission does not expressly exclude national
political committees, neither does it expressly list national political committees as
entities which can establish Proposition 73 qualified recipient committees, and there is
nothing to indicate that Proposition 73 intended to limit its scope to only state and local
party units. It also should be noted that the term "political party" appears but is
nowhere defined in California campaign finance law. See. e.g., Cal. Code 985302 and
85303O).
Dwight 0. Eisenhower Republican Conter.310 First Slreet Southeast. Washington, D.C.20003. (202) 863-8638
Telex: 701144 . FAX: (202) 863-8820


Accordingly, our specihc inquiry is whether the RNC, as a national party committee,
may establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under Section 18539. If
you have any questions, please contact me at (202) 863-8638.
BLG:ljd

sections.question: Whether the Republican National Committee (RNC), as a national party committee, may establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18539.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the Republican National Committee, as a national political party committee, establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under California regulation 18539, which currently permits state and local committees to do so but is silent on national committees?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the RNC to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present. Document contains clear QUESTION section. No section headers found as noted in parsing notes, but question content is clearly identifiable from letter body.

embedding.summary: The Republican National Committee requests an FPPC advice letter regarding whether it, as a national party committee, may establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under California regulation 18539, noting that the regulation expressly permits state and local committees but is silent on national committees.
